description: Stream Live IPTV, Video On-Demand, and Series from an Xtream-compatible server using this plugin.
name: Jellyfin Xtream
overview: Stream content from an Xtream-compatible server.

versions.changelog:
### Fixes
- **SPS NAL-based keyframe seeking**: Scan for H.264 SPS NAL units (type 7) instead of unreliable RAI flags — many IPTV encoders don't set RAI
- **2MB pre-buffer** (was 256KB): At 20Mbps, 256KB is only 0.1s — now buffers ~0.8s to reliably capture a keyframe
- Should eliminate 'non-existing PPS 0 referenced' decode errors at stream start

### Fixes
- **Keyframe-aligned stream start**: Scan buffer for MPEG-TS RAI flag before serving — eliminates ~140 'non-existing PPS 0 referenced' decode errors at stream start
- **Channel probe on Live TV selection** (removed from TV Overrides page)
- **Pre-buffer 256KB** for reliable stream probing on Android TV

### Channel Probe Feature
- 🔍 Probe button on Live TV channel selection shows codec, resolution, framerate, audio codec
- Direct play compatibility per client (Android TV, Samsung Tizen, LG webOS, Xbox, Web)
- Also available on TV Channel Overrides page

### Direct Play Fix
- Pre-buffer 256KB in Restream.Open() so Jellyfin's stream probe gets real video data
- Fixes Height:0 / Level:-99 probe failures that prevented direct play on Android TV
- Keyframe-aligned segment splitting for clean HLS playback

## What's Changed

- **HLS direct play**: Serve live TV as HLS playlists with `EXT-X-DISCONTINUITY` markers for PTS jump handling
- **Direct play** on Android TV, Samsung Tizen, LG webOS, Xbox — no remux needed
- Web clients get lossless remux (`-codec copy`) automatically (Jellyfin design constraint)
- Removed `IDirectStreamProvider`, `TsTimestampRewriter`, `WrappedBufferStream` — simplified to pure HLS segment serving
- **Fixed stale playlist bug** causing replay when all segments are pruned
- Added 6 ChannelBuffer unit tests for replay detection and playlist correctness
- 23 total tests passing

Replace ffmpeg remuxer with in-process TsTimestampRewriter for zero-latency PTS/DTS/PCR normalization and continuity counter fixup. Wall-clock-based discontinuity adjustment prevents PTS drift during round-robin multiplexing. Null TS packet gap-fill keeps transcoder alive without backward timestamps. Add round-robin crash protection with error recovery. 13 unit tests.

Optimize prebuffer and round-robin for stable playback

- Use all available segments as prebuffer for larger initial burst
- Increase Open wait to 5 segments
- Reduce yield to 4 for faster round-robin cycling
- Add -err_detect ignore_err to remuxer
- Add remuxer exit code logging

## Changes in v0.9.44.0

- Fix non-monotonic DTS causing playback stutter (removed -break_non_keyframes)
- Revert probesize from 500K to 1M (eliminates 0-segment captures)
- Consistent yield at 4 segments (no premature starving yield)
- 0-segment capture detection and logging
- Pump poll reduced to 100ms, WaitForExit 500ms
- All 60 integration tests passing

### Changes
- **Persistent parallel captures**: Replaced blocking Task.WhenAll with per-channel capture tasks that start/stop dynamically
- **Fair round-robin yield**: Reduced yield thresholds (starvation: 2, default: 3 segments) to account for ~8s ffmpeg startup overhead
- **Parallel→sequential transition**: Clean handoff when channels are added/removed
- **Lifecycle cleanup**: Capture tasks properly cancelled on unsubscribe, stop, and dispose

### Test coverage (45 assertions)
- Tests 1-6: Single/dual recording, keepalive, HLS playback, ffprobe/ffmpeg validation
- Test 7: HLS discontinuity tags during round-robin
- Test 8: Parallel stream fairness (segment ratio within 3:1)
- Test 9: Staggered start — parallel-to-sequential transition

All tests pass with maxConnections=1 and real DW News HLS streams.

Fix HLS discontinuity tags in recording playlist.

- Insert #EXT-X-DISCONTINUITY between capture rounds (fixes ffmpeg exit 234)
- 7 integration tests, 34 assertions, all pass

## Changes

- Fix recording playback failure (ffmpeg exit 234): delay library notification until 3 HLS segments are ready so ffprobe has enough data to analyze
- Fix recording file cleanup so .ts, .strm, and segment files are always removed
- Add ffprobe/ffmpeg validation to integration tests (28 assertions)

## Changes

- Switch recording playback from TS tailing to HLS playlist approach for reliable seeking and playback during multiplexer gaps
- Fix timer restart bug where cancelled recordings were re-started by CheckTimers
- Fix recording file cleanup so intermediate .ts, .strm, and segment files are always removed
- Add integration test suite (21 assertions covering single/dual recordings, stream keepalive, HLS playback, cleanup)

- **Fix recording seeking**: Inject #EXT-X-ENDLIST so hls.js treats playlist as VOD → full backward seeking from segment 0
- #EXT-X-START:TIME-OFFSET=-12 positions player near live edge on initial load
- Direct-play HLS (no transcoding) for recording channels
- Remove [Produces] from recording playlist endpoint

## Fixes

**Multiplexer:**
- Fix 406 error on playlist endpoint — removed `[Produces]` attribute that blocked ASP.NET from serving the HLS playlist. This caused ffmpeg to exit with code 8.

**Recording seekbar:**
- Serve ffmpeg EVENT playlist as-is (no more `#EXT-X-ENDLIST` injection)
- HLS.js now treats recording as live → positions at live edge, matching the Jellyfin guide overlay
- All segments from the beginning are available for backward seeking
- Use actual schedule times for programme (not stale `DateTime.UtcNow`)

## Fix recording seekbar alignment

The live TV player positions the seekbar at `now - programme.StartDate`. Previously `StartDate` was the original schedule time, so the seekbar jumped to the live edge.

**Changes:**
- `StartDate = now` → seekbar starts at position 0 (beginning of content)
- `EndDate = now + elapsed recording duration` → only shows available content, not the full EPG slot
- `RunTimeTicks` set on MediaSource to match elapsed duration

## Experimental Stream Multiplexer

Round-robin a single IPTV connection across multiple live channels by capturing short HLS segments per channel. Enables watching multiple streams with a single provider connection.

### New features
- **ConnectionMultiplexer** — IHostedService that cycles through subscribed channels
- **Per-channel HLS buffers** with discontinuity markers and bounded retention
- **Config UI** — enable/disable multiplexing, slice duration, retention window
- Off by default. Enable in plugin settings under "Stream Multiplexer".

### Trade-off
Latency increases with the number of channels: N channels × slice seconds behind live.

Fix recording seekbar: bypass Jellyfin transcoder via direct-play HLS. The plugin HLS endpoint now serves VOD-style playlist (#EXT-X-ENDLIST) so HLS.js starts from position 0 instead of the live edge.

## Fix end time

Programme window: `[now, scheduledEnd]` — seekbar shows the correct remaining recording duration.

Combined with IDirectStreamProvider from v0.9.25 which guarantees data starts from byte 0 (beginning of recording).

As you watch, seekbar and video advance 1:1.

## Fix seekbar alignment

**Two key changes:**

1. **Programme duration = elapsed recording time** — the seekbar range now matches the actual content in the TS file. Previously it showed remaining schedule time which was shorter than the recorded content, preventing forward seeking.

2. **IDirectStreamProvider** — RecordingRestream now serves the TS file through Jellyfin's LiveStreamFiles endpoint via TailingFileStream (like Restream does). This guarantees data is always served from byte 0 (beginning of recording) regardless of how Jellyfin handles file protocol internally.

## Fix programme end time

Programme end was `now + totalDuration` which overshot past the actual scheduled end. Now uses `scheduledEnd` directly so the seekbar window is `[now, scheduledEnd]` = remaining recording time.

`RunTimeTicks` on the MediaSource also reflects remaining duration to stay in sync.

## Fix seekbar position sync

The live TV player calculates seekbar position as `now - programme.StartDate`. When the programme used the original schedule (e.g. yesterday 19:00), the offset was hours off.

Now the programme is anchored at the current time with the full duration, so the player calculates `now - now = 0` → seekbar starts at the beginning, matching the actual video.

**Trade-off**: Timeline shows current time range instead of original EPG times. Original schedule is shown in the programme description.

## Fix seekbar time correlation

Set `IsLive=false` on the virtual recording programme. The live TV player was positioning the seekbar at wall clock "now" instead of the actual playback position. Now the seekbar correctly starts at the programme start time and reflects where you actually are in the recording.

## Recording channels: start from beginning + full seekbar

Switched from HTTP HLS URL to local TS file for recording channel playback:

- **Starts from the beginning** — ffmpeg reads the TS file from byte 0
- **Full EPG timeslot in seekbar** — `RunTimeTicks` set to scheduled duration (with padding)
- **Seeking works** — ffmpeg can use `-ss` on the local file when you scrub the timeline

## Seeking and start-from-beginning for recording channels

- **Start from beginning**: Injects `#EXT-X-START:TIME-OFFSET=0` into the HLS playlist so playback starts from the first segment instead of the live edge.
- **Seeking support**: Enables `SupportsTranscoding` and `SupportsDirectStream` so Jellyfin can restart the transcode with `-ss` when seeking.
- **Media stream info**: Declares h264/aac streams so Jellyfin can make proper direct-play vs transcode decisions.

## Absolute seekbar for recording channels

Sets `RunTimeTicks` on the recording media source based on the timer schedule (including pre/post padding), so the player shows the full scheduled duration instead of a relative 0-based seekbar.

## Fix recording playback URL mismatch

**Bug:** `RecordingRestream` constructed HLS URLs as `/playlist.m3u8` but the API controller serves at `/stream.m3u8`. This caused ffmpeg exit code 8 when clients tried to watch active recordings via the virtual recording channel.

**Fix:** Aligned URLs to `/stream.m3u8` in `RecordingRestream.cs`.

### Changes
- **Seekable recording channels**: Virtual LiveTV channels now use HLS direct play instead of raw TS pipe. Clients play the m3u8 playlist natively (HLS.js on web, ExoPlayer on Android, AVPlayer on iOS) with full seeking support.
- **Guide auto-refresh**: Channel list and guide refresh automatically when recordings start and stop. Virtual `● REC:` channels appear within seconds.
- **CORS fix**: Added Access-Control-Allow-Origin header to HLS segment endpoint.
- `IsInfiniteStream = false` — clients get a proper seek bar.

### How it works
The recording channel MediaSource points directly at the HLS playlist API URL. No IDirectStreamProvider (no raw byte pipe). The client's native HLS player handles seeking across segments.

Fix: Recording streams now start from the beginning of the file instead of jumping to the live edge.

Changed `EnableStreamSharing` to `false` so each consumer gets their own independent `TailingFileStream` starting from byte 0.

### Changes
- Active recordings now appear as virtual LiveTV channels in the guide
- Channels labeled **● REC: {name}** with priority channel numbers (appear at top)
- Uses IDirectStreamProvider (same as live TV) for reliable growing playback on all clients
- EPG program entry shows wall-clock times from the timer (enables time-based seek bar)
- Reverted .strm approach which caused transcoding/crashes on web
- .ts file remains in library for post-recording playback
- Removed the stream.ts HTTP endpoint (replaced by LiveTV approach)

### Known limitations
- Virtual channels appear on next guide refresh (may take a few minutes after recording starts)
- Seeking within the recording stream is not yet supported (treated as infinite live stream)

Fix duplicate library entries and stream termination

- Move growing TS file into hidden .rec_* directory (only .strm visible in library)
- Clean up orphaned .ts files from previous versions on startup
- EPG time window in filenames (e.g. Show_20260423_20.00-20.44)

Growing TS stream endpoint for continuous recording playback

- Recordings now use a .strm file pointing to a growing TS stream endpoint
- Stream continues growing while watching (no more termination at playback start)
- Fixed cleanup order and merge-failure handling
- Improved file drain logic and reduced poll interval
- Windows compatibility fix (FileShare.Delete)
